channel.title: Jornal TRIBUNA DE MACAU EN
channel.description: Jornal TRIBUNA DE MACAU EN RSS

item.title: Only six social houses available for more than a thousand single-person households
item.description:
More than two thousand people are on the waiting list for social housing, data from the Housing Institute reveal. Although the houses available for allocation cover 54% of the candidates, the truth is that there are 1,106 single-person households and only six T1 houses are available. The government body told TRIBUNA DE MACAU newspaper that it is not allocating apartments of a higher type to single-element households
Catarina Pereira
There are 2,003 people on the waiting list for social housing, according to data from the Housing Institute (IH) up until yesterday. There were 1,077 fractions available for allocation, which could cover 54% of the candidates waiting. Part of the existing, but unavailable, fractions are under repair, but the majority are still in the construction process. The organization's statistics consulted by the TRIBUNA DE MACAU newspaper reveal, however, that single-person households represent the majority of those waiting for social homes – and the T1 fractions do not even reach a dozen. Specifically, 1,106 families that are on the waiting list are made up of just one member, making up 55% of the total. However, of the 1,077 units available for allocation, only six are T1. This newspaper questioned the government body about whether it would eventually be allocating houses of a type higher than T1 to households of just one person. In the response, IH points out that, since the implementation of the permanent application for social housing and in accordance with the regulations of the legal regime for social housing, “households made up of one member are only allocated fractions of type T0/T0I or T1”. For this reason, IH “is not allocating any fraction of social housing type T2 or higher to single-person households that are on the waiting list”. There are more one-bedroom apartments, totaling 121, but they are under repair. Restoration works include painting, replacing doors and windows, repairing electricity and water supply and drainage installations, among others. On the other hand, of the apartments that are under construction, the majority are T1, totaling 3,276, but you have to wait for them to be finished. Of the more than two thousand people waiting for a house, around 76%, that is, 1,523 have not yet been notified to present the documents for assessment before the allocation of a unit. It means that only 480 have already been notified for this procedure. After family units made up of one member, there are those with two members (661), three people (205) and four members (23). There are also eight candidates whose groups are made up of five or more people. As for the age range of representatives from each household, the majority are between 45 and 64 years old (760), between 23 and 44 (738) and 65 or more years old (492). There are only 13 households whose representatives are between 18 and 22 years of age. As for the housing units available for allocation, in addition to T1, there are 981 T2 houses and 90 T3 and T4 houses. The fractions that are being repaired are 437, including 121 T1s, 230 T2 houses and 86 T3 and T4 apartments. Regarding social housing under construction, and in addition to the T1 apartments already mentioned, there are 672 T2 houses and 140 T3 units. In total, the 4,088 units under construction are all located in Zone A of the New Landfills. IH statistics also show that, until the beginning of March, there were a total of 15,427 social housing tenants, of which 14,810 had incomes not exceeding the maximum limit. There were also 609 with an income not exceeding twice the maximum limit and two with an income exceeding twice the maximum limit. Regarding the composition of households, 10,844 were made up of one to two people; 3,956 had three to four members; 591 were made up of five to six people; and only 36 were families of seven or more people. Household representatives are mainly elderly, as 8,395 are aged 65 or over. Furthermore, 4,577 are between 45 and 64 years old, 2,442 are between 23 and 44 years old and only 13 are between 18 and 22 years old. Other data also reveals that, until the beginning of March, 11 cases were filed on suspicion of people not using social housing as a permanent residence or allowing people not included in the contract to live there. In addition, two cases were filed for recording violations of the building regulations by the tenant.
Government is reviewing support from the Building Repair Fund The administrative regulations of the Building Repair Fund and financial support plans are being reviewed, said the president of the Housing Institute (IH), Iam Lei Leng, in response to a question from deputy Wong Kit Cheng. As he explained, the objective is to “expand the scope of subsidized projects and the financing limits, supporting owners in carrying out repair work on buildings”. Remember that requests for repairs to class P and M buildings can now be made online, through the Single Account or the Platform for Companies and Associations. IH then intends to gradually expand the types of services provided online.

item.title: First Store Support Plan received more than 40 applications
item.description:
The first phase of the plan aimed at supporting the establishment of the First Store in Macau received more than 40 applications from countries and regions such as China, Portugal, Thailand, Hong Kong and Taiwan, with particular emphasis on firms dedicated to new business models and technological elements. The second phase will accept proposals from May 1st
VÍTOR REBELO
The first phase of the “Plan for Economic Development to Support the Establishment of the First Store in Macau” has been completed, with the entity promoting the initiative, the Trade and Investment Promotion Institute (IPIM), having received more than 600 requests for information, as well as more than 40 applications. The brands come from different countries and regions, namely Mainland China, Portugal, Thailand, Hong Kong and Taiwan, with particular emphasis on brands dedicated to new business models and technological elements, such as games with interactive motion detection elements and product experience centers. Approximately 87% of applications are the result of collaboration between Macau companies and foreign brands, while almost 46% aim to establish the first stores in the ZAPE, Barca, Areia Preta and other designated areas. In accordance with what is stipulated in the Plan, IPIM carried out the analysis of the proposals presented, as well as the evaluation of the candidate brands through the Investment Committee, “with a view to evaluating and approving the applications on a case-by-case basis according to the weighting factors, such as budgetary capacity and the public interest in general, as well as the circumstances that condition each process”, according to a statement from the organization. IPIM emphasizes that financial support will only be granted after verification and confirmation of applications that meet all requirements and have obtained brand recognition and duly presented documents proving operation and expenses within the stipulated deadline, in accordance with the principles of appropriate use of public funds and reimbursement of actual expenses. Launched in 2025, the Plan, which lasts three years and is divided into six phases, aims to support the establishment of the first store in the territory, in order to “increase the attractiveness of Macau's business environment, enriching the city's tourist offer, boosting the economy of community neighborhoods and introducing more services and new business models, with a view to promoting the diversification of the local market”, highlights the note. In addition to providing financial support, the additional scheme was established to encourage foreign brands to give priority to establishing the first store in the eight designated areas (namely Ilha Verde, Fai Chi Kei, Doca do Lamau, Tamagnini Barbosa, Areia Preta and Iao Hon, Barca, Barra/Manduco and ZAPE) and to hire a certain number of full-time local workers. The objective is to “contribute to the development of Macau’s community areas and create more employment opportunities”, highlights IPIM. The maximum amount to be awarded per case is one million patacas. Applications for the second phase of the plan will be open between May 1 and July 31, 2026.

item.title: Macau Forum intends to maintain “good pace of work”
item.description:
Ji Xianzheng, secretary-general of the Permanent Secretariat of Forum Macau, stated that he counts on the media to “spread to the world the good stories of cooperation between China and the PLP, with Macau as a platform”. The person in charge also said he hopes that last year’s “good pace of work” will extend to 2026.
PEDRO MILHEIRÃO For this year, the Permanent Secretariat of the Forum for Economic and Trade Cooperation between China and Portuguese-speaking Countries (PLP) intends to “maintain the good pace of work in 2025”, and “actively promote” the implementation of the Action Plan for Economic and Trade Cooperation, Ji Xianzheng, secretary-general of the organization, announced yesterday. During a spring lunch, Ji also asked the media to continue “telling the world the good stories of cooperation between China and the PLP, with Macau as a platform”. In a review of the activities carried out in 2025, Ji began by saying that it was “a year of intense and very concrete work for the Permanent Secretariat”, adding that “all the main work progressed in a stable manner”. Throughout 2025, the organization received “more than 100 delegations”, including “seven ministerial and provincial level leaders from Mainland China, and nine senior representatives of the PLP, including a Head of State”, referring to the official visit of Umaro Embaló, president of Guinea-Bissau. For the secretary-general, the Forum's headquarters “has become a practically obligatory stopping point for delegations from all over visiting Macau”. “We participated in 63 relevant economic and commercial activities carried out in Macau and Mainland China, and carried out official visits to two PLPs, Equatorial Guinea and Guinea-Bissau”, he noted. Furthermore, cooperation agreements were signed with the Provinces of Heilongjiang and Qinghai, and five lectures were organized on tourism, blue economy, infrastructure development, digital economy and training of small and medium-sized companies, in which 86 staff and technicians from the PLP and China participated. He also highlighted the fact that the 17th Cultural Week of China and the PLP was extended to Mainland China for the first time, taking place in Beijing and Zhongshan, in addition to Macau. Ji Xianzheng also praised the “very valuable contribution” of the media in publicizing the work of Forum Macau and promoting cooperation and “friendship between China and the Portuguese-speaking area”. This year, the Forum will hold five new talks and will continue with more “exchange actions and mutual visits with Mainland China and the PLP”. In addition, it will once again “take Cultural Week to Mainland China” and deepen “comprehensive and mutually beneficial cooperation between China and the PLP”, said Ji.
Beijing and Qinghai may host Cultural Week On the sidelines of the event, Ji Xianzheng told journalists that this year's edition of Cultural Week should take place between the “last week of June and the beginning of July”. The work is being coordinated with the municipality of Beijing and the Government of Qinghai, with a view to carrying out the initiative in these two regions, in addition to Macau. Referring to the sessions of the 14th National People's Congress and the 14th National Committee of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference, Ji Xianzheng said that "a very important political message was conveyed to boost comprehensive cooperation between China and the PLP" and "a very important institutional strengthening" for the Permanent Secretariat, without revealing details. Asked whether the inclusion of the development of cooperation with Spanish-speaking countries, something mentioned in recent official speeches, could affect the connection with Portuguese-speaking countries, Ji replied that “the Permanent Secretariat of Forum Macau works primarily with Portuguese-speaking Countries”. “The rest is the responsibility of [other] Macau institutions”.

item.title: DSEDJ invests in counseling agents
item.description:
Indicating that it has increased the costs of staff services for teams of student counseling agents by around 3% this academic year, DSEDJ states that it is committed to continuing to improve the conditions of these professionals, who currently number 413. In this sense, the organization says that it will “continuously review and analyze the configuration of human resources and the service model of student counseling teams”, striving to stabilize them
Catarina Pereira
The Directorate of Education and Youth Development Services (DSEDJ) highlighted that “investment in resources directed towards student counseling teams has been increasing”. Specifically, he indicated that, in the 2025/2026 academic year, “the costs for the services of the staff of these teams increased by around 3%, having increased their number to 413 people”. In response to a question from deputy Nick Lei, signed by the deputy director, Iun Pui Iun, the organization recalls that, to further reinforce complementary resources and alleviate the workload of student counseling agents outside office hours, it created an open student counseling hotline and an online consultation platform, “in order to provide 24-hour professional counseling services, monitoring students' needs through online and in-person collaboration”. “DSEDJ established student counseling teams, clearly defining the levels of professional career development of these agents, so that the counseling services can develop in a professional manner”, he continued, highlighting that, currently, these teams, in addition to the agents, also include elements with the functions of coordinator and deputy coordinator, itinerant psychological counseling agents for students with experience, professional inspectors and activity assistants. The objective is to “increase the effectiveness of services through collaboration between the different positions of the team”. “Through collaboration between these different functions, the teams provide collective support to front-line employees in dealing with cases of students with special needs or complex circumstances, also including their respective families”, says the organization, in another response, to Ngan Iek Hang, on the same matter. Looking to the future, DSEDJ ensures that it will “continuously review and analyze the configuration of human resources and the service model of the student counseling teams, striving to stabilize them, in order to provide students with more professional and comprehensive services and support”. With regard to strengthening the professional guarantees of counseling agents, the DSEDJ also indicates that it increased, in the current academic year, the allocation of resources for expenses with staff services, “so that institutions can define a salary update mechanism according to their seniority and performance”. Institutions are therefore encouraged to “provide opportunities for promotion to different jobs within the advisory team”, to “strengthen the retention and transmission of experiences of experienced agents”. The organization also established a training framework aimed at counseling institutions, indicating training hours, “aimed at encouraging them to provide training activities, in line with the aforementioned framework, for their agents”. In the same response it is also said that DSEDJ organizes, annually, a ceremony to award praise to the student counseling teams, to recognize the work they carry out.

item.title: War leaves airlines under increasing pressure
item.description:
Asia-Pacific airlines are making major changes to their operations in response to the conflict in the Middle East and the closure of airspace in the Persian Gulf, a common route for intercontinental flights. Thus, the region's main carriers were forced to change routes, reduce flight frequency or increase prices. The costs of some connections between Asia and Europe have 'skyrocketed', with flights that normally cost between 300 and 600 dollars now exceeding 1,000 and even 2,000 dollars, according to values ​​recorded on “Google Flights” for popular routes such as Hanoi-Paris, Singapore-London and Tokyo-Madrid. This is due to airspace congestion on new routes and the almost total interruption of crude oil traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, which has driven up prices, especially in Asia, the region most dependent on imports from this strategic route. “The usual Europe-Asia corridor, which crosses Iran, Iraq and the Persian Gulf, has disappeared, and airlines are forced to avoid it, heading north via the Caucasus/Afghanistan or south via Egypt/Saudi Arabia/Oman, which means longer flight times, greater fuel consumption and greater pressure on the remaining corridors,” David Mumford, a specialist in international air operations at the “Ops Group”, told EFE. Several Chinese carriers have announced measures to relax conditions for changes or cancellations without penalty on certain routes to the Middle East. Some, like Air China, chose to redesign routes and, after canceling stops in Dubai and Doha, are now flying over Russian airspace, closed to Western carriers due to sanctions imposed after the war in Ukraine, according to data from “Flightradar”, a platform that tracks flights around the world. Other companies, such as Singapore Airlines and Cathay Pacific, which initially announced the suspension of operations in the region, opted for routes further north, flying over Central Asia via Uzbekistan, Kazakhstan and Turkmenistan, and the Southern Ural Mountains, Azerbaijan, Armenia and Turkey, on their way to Europe or the Americas. In this context, both Turkish Airlines and Istanbul will be the big winners, predicts Andrew Charlton, general director of “Aviation Advocacy”, an air transport consultancy. Charlton highlights the potential of Ankara, “given its location, its huge new central airport, its local population and the fact that it can operate the left side of its airline network”. According to Eurocontrol, a European air navigation safety organization, two Turkish airports, Kayseri Mil and Bodrum, are among the five with the highest percentage increase in flights this month compared to 2025. Australian Qantas has changed its popular direct flight between Perth and London, which will now include a technical stopover in Singapore, as the need to avoid conflict prevents the aircraft from carrying enough fuel to complete the journey without stopovers. The airspace of most Middle Eastern countries has remained closed since February 28, when the US and Israel attacked Iran, and Tehran retaliated against targets across the region.
Travel and tourism sector loses 600 million dollars per day The World Travel and Tourism Council (WTTC) estimated a serious impact on the travel sector due to the conflict in the Middle East, with losses of 600 million dollars per day. As WTTC highlighted yesterday, the Middle East plays a “vital” role in global travel, as the region represents 5% of international arrivals worldwide and 14% of international transit traffic. Therefore, any interruption affects demand around the world, which impacts airports and flights, hotels, car rental companies and cruise lines. Despite the current challenges, the WTTC emphasized that the travel and tourism sector is one of the most resilient economic sectors in the world, with previous crises demonstrating that tourist demand following security-related incidents, “with the appropriate response” can recover in just two months if governments and industry act quickly to restore traveler confidence.
JTM with international agencies

item.title: Awards seek excellence in the tourism sector
item.description:
More prizes, some monetary, were added to the Quality Tourism Services Assessment Program. The restaurant and travel agency sectors are among those covered. According to the deputy director of DST, Jennifer Si Tou, the initiative “promotes the diversification and high-quality and sustainable development of the tourism industry”
In keeping with the times, as stated in a statement from the Directorate of Tourism Services (DST), the 2026 Quality Tourist Services Assessment Program, based on existing awards for the catering and travel agency sectors, will add to the Gold distinctions, also Silver and Bronze, “with a view to encouraging more companies to participate and continually raise the quality of their development”. Furthermore, the Courtesy Company Award was extended to the restaurant sector so that “more companies are encouraged to implement, in practice, an attitude of hospitality, to jointly build an environment of high quality tourist services”, highlights DST. On the other hand, from this year onwards, the catering sector will optimize the scope of application, with the categories of participating companies having been adjusted. “Through the merger and simplification of the category structure, the program aims to meet the needs of future development”, highlights the organization. The program will award in 2026 the Quality Tourist Services Company Award, the Gold, Silver and Bronze Quality Tourist Services Award, the Excellence Award and the Special Theme Award. There will also be monetary prizes, with 10,000 patacas being awarded to the participating company in each category with the highest score, while the second and third will receive 8,000 and 6,000, respectively. Companies that win for three consecutive years will be given a check for 20,000. patacas. In a promotional seminar to present the process of recruitment, evaluation and awarding of prizes, as well as how establishments can participate, the deputy director of DST, Jennifer Si Tou, said that, together with tour operators, the aim is to “raise and consolidate the quality of tourist services in Macau”, in an initiative that “promotes the diversification and high-quality and sustainable development of the tourism industry”. Launched in 2014, the program continues this year with no limit on registrations for the restaurant and travel agency sectors. All establishments that apply by March 31st, and that meet the necessary requirements, are admitted and proceed to the evaluation phase. Admitted candidate establishments will be subject to evaluation of services by mystery shopping and audit of the service management system. DST will hold a clarification session on the evaluation criteria for successful competitors.
V.R.

item.title: Guards suspected of helping inmates smuggle contraband
item.description:
The Commissioner against Corruption (CCAC) referred two cases, involving an active guard and another retired, to the Public Prosecutor's Office on suspicion of having helped inmates smuggle products into the Prison. There are more people involved in these cases. According to a press release, the organization discovered that an active Correctional Services prison guard “accepted bribes”, while the retired guard “abused his power”, having both “helped two inmates to smuggle products”. Following the investigation, it was discovered that “the active prison guard took advantage of his duties to help an inmate smuggle products into the prison area, and, after such objects reached that inmate, he looked for opportunities to sell them in the prison for profit”. The inmate in question, through his wife, paid the prison guard an amount of at least 17 thousand patacas in return for the help. The CCAC also found that this guard “had already committed speeding offenses twice, and, to avoid responsibility, he had resorted to a family member and a friend to take the blame”. This guard is suspected of committing the crimes of passive corruption for an illicit act and document forgery provided for in the Penal Code. The crime is punishable by a prison sentence of one to eight years. The inmate involved and his wife are suspected of committing the crime of active corruption, punishable by up to three years in prison or a fine. In turn, the family member and friend, who took the blame as “scapegoats” on behalf of the prison guard, are suspected of committing the crime of document forgery, punishable by up to three years in prison or a fine. As for the second prison guard, currently retired, the CCAC discovered that “he had provided assistance, during the time he was carrying out his duties, to another inmate in smuggling products into the prison establishment”. Furthermore, it helped him exchange messages, “secretly and several times”, with his family. According to the organization, the active prison guard is suspected of abuse of power.
CP.

item.title: Authorities warn of the risk of transmission of both fevers
item.description:
Authorities expect that, from April onwards, there will be a “constant increase” in the risk of transmission of dengue and Chikungunya fevers
The Working Group for the Prevention of Dengue Fever predicts that, from April onwards, there will be “a constant increase in the risk of cross-border transmission of the two fevers”, dengue and Chikungunya, as well as their “community spread”. “Therefore, it is necessary to reinforce working arrangements to prevent and control these diseases together”, he highlighted, at a working meeting chaired by representatives of the Health Services and the Institute for Municipal Affairs. In a summary, it was said that Macau recorded 46 cases of Chikungunya fever (38 imported, 8 local) and 36 cases of dengue fever (35 imported, 1 local) over the past year. “The data shows a serious situation of dengue fever throughout the world and in the regions neighboring Macau”, warned the Group. Therefore, it assessed the risk of transmission of the two fevers during this year and discussed the implementation of various prevention, control and awareness work, namely planning a series of inspection actions, eliminating the source of proliferation and work to eliminate mosquitoes. At the same time, activities were carried out in community neighborhoods to raise awareness. According to a statement, “it was concluded that the most effective method for eliminating the proliferation of mosquitoes and larvae is to remove stagnant water”, so the Group called on the public to take “three anti-mosquito measures”, with a view to preventing infectious diseases transmitted by them: eliminate stagnant water; use mosquito nets; and protect yourself from bites outdoors. The Working Group guarantees that “it will remain attentive to the situation of the two fevers in the neighboring regions of Macau, to ensure good response preparation”. He also appealed to the population that, in addition to eliminating stagnant water at home or at work, they should also follow preventive measures when traveling abroad. “If you feel unwell after returning to Macau, and if you present symptoms suspicious of dengue fever or Chikungunya fever, such as fever or rash, you should seek medical assistance immediately”, he adds. Furthermore, it reminds healthcare professionals to be alert to patients suspected of having both fevers. Meanwhile, the SSM reported an imported case of Chikungunya fever, the second case imported and registered in the territory this year. This is a 20-year-old female resident who traveled alone to the Philippines. She is “clinically stable”, but admitted to the Conde de São Januário Hospital Center to receive treatment.

item.title: IC program once again supports cinematographic productions
item.description: Until April 10th, applications are open for the “The Power of Image” program, organized by the Cultural Institute (IC). The plan returns to financing film productions by local residents. The objective is to “promote the development of cinematographic art in Macau”, says the organization. The program is divided into three categories: Documentary, Fiction Short Film and Animation, each subdivided into levels: Advanced, Free and Beginners. The IC will invite professionals to join the jury, which will select a maximum of 16 projects, which will be awarded a total amount of up to 1.55 million patacas, depending on the category and level, according to the organization's statement. In turn, each selected project may receive, individually, a maximum value of 280,000 patacas for production costs. The selected participants will also receive guidance from professionals, “who will provide individual advice to each production team”. In addition to holding a BIR, candidates must be 18 or over and meet the requirements set out in the regulations, which can be consulted on the IC website. With a view to “increasing professionalism, market competitiveness and international visibility of selected short films”, this year the program introduces new specific support measures. Specifically, explains the IC, from among the short films selected, “the jury will select a maximum of three outstanding works, whose participants will be entitled to strategic planning services for film festivals and international commercial promotion, for a period of one year”. Candidates must submit the form and necessary documents to the IC Building, Praça do Tap Siac, Macau, by 5:30 pm on April 10th or send the scanned documents by email ([email protected]) by 11:59 pm on the same day.

item.title: RUC celebrates 40-year legacy with 11 DJs
item.description: Rádio Universidade de Coimbra (RUC) celebrates its 40th anniversary tomorrow, “with a night dedicated to music, memories and community”, in the “DD3 Verandah” space at “Fisherman’s Wharf”, according to a press release from “None of Your Business” (NOYB). As an integral part of the Rota das Letras Literary Festival program, the celebration will bring together 11 local and international DJs. The night dedicated “to the sounds that defined the RUC over four decades” will feature the artists “Kobayashi”, “Oba”, “The Undyker” (Valério Romão), “Loyal Dragon”, “IDego”, “Prank Sinatra”, “Tongrim”, “G Tat”, “fer.nandoM”, “DJ Soneca” and “Bate-Ficha”. NOYB invites all interested parties “to participate in a moment of shared celebration”. “More than a party, the event will bring together those who grew up listening to the sounds of 107.9 FM and anyone who wants to join in a night of conviviality, energy and connections created by music”, says the note. The organization promises “a vibrant atmosphere where nostalgia meets discovery, in a space to celebrate the legacy of the radio station that formed generations through music, creativity and independent voices”. The RUC's annual celebrations have become a tradition in Macau since 2016, when former students from the University of Coimbra who lived in Macau organized the first event to mark the anniversary in the territory. In fact, many of these students were involved with RUC during their academic careers. Since the first edition, the initiative has continued to grow as a meeting point for former students, friends and music enthusiasts, according to NOYB. The event will start at 11pm and continue into the night, with entry costing 180 patacas, with three drinks included. Interested parties will be able to find more information on the event's social networks, namely Facebook and Instagram.

item.title: AMCM guarantees to be attentive to “injured” financial products
item.description: The Macau Monetary Authority (AMCM) told the newspaper TRIBUNA DE MACAU that it was attaching “the greatest importance” to a “complaint presented by a citizen about financial wealth management products (private placement) sold by a bank”. The organization also added that it has already started the investigation process. The response comes after deputy José Pereira Coutinho accompanied, as he told this newspaper, seven residents to the AMCM to lodge a complaint “for having suffered losses in the acquisition of financial products from a bank in Macau, worth more than 208 million patacas”. “It is the tip of a huge ‘iceberg’ of high-risk products and the use of unethical methods by senior management at the banking institution, [who] enticed Macau residents to invest in these products”, said the deputy. Asked about how many people were harmed and the total amount involved, AMCM said it had no additional information to provide. “AMCM, within the scope of its legal responsibilities, has been carrying out rigorous supervision over the operations and management of banking institutions, ensuring the soundness of their risk management and compliance with legal and regulatory standards in their administration, with the aim of safeguarding the legitimate rights and interests of depositors and investors”, he guaranteed. Therefore, he added, “if irregular practices carried out by financial institutions are confirmed, AMCM, using its powers, will adopt the corresponding appropriate supervisory measures”.

item.title: BRIEF
item.description:
Macao SAR recovers land destined for green zone The Government recovered State land in Rampa dos Cavaleiros, following joint interdepartmental action. According to the Directorate of Land and Urban Construction Services (DSSCU), the lot is part of a set of 39 plots of land that the Government intends to provisionally use, and will be allocated to “a green area and open public space, with the widening of the sidewalks”. With an area of ​​around 695 square meters, it contained an illegal construction with metal supports and awnings, and was fenced with metal siding, concrete walls and nets where containers and mechanical equipment had been placed. DSSCU notified the illegal occupants to vacate and reverse the lot. After the deadline, and since the land had not been completely vacated and returned, the authorities proceeded with its recovery.
DSAT launches tender for six auto-silos The Traffic Affairs Bureau (DSAT) opened a public tender for the concession of operating the parking lots at Jardim de Iao Hon, Rua da Bacia Sul, Macau Cultural Center, Rotunda da Concordia, Macau Union Hospital and Mong Son Building. The deadline for submitting proposals is 5pm on April 15th. The public competition ceremony will take place in the DSAT meeting room, the following day, at 9:30 am.
Conventions and exhibitions promoted in Thailand Representatives from Macau conventions and exhibitions participated in the “Asia-Pacific Conference of the Global Exhibition Industry Association (UFI)” in Bangkok, during which the advantages of the sector in Macau and Hengqin were promoted. At the session, the president of the Trade and Investment Promotion Institute, Che Weng Keong, said that the Macao SAR “has dedicated efforts to training qualified staff and increasing the level of operators in the sector, creating more business opportunities”. The Macau Trade and Exhibition Association and UFI signed the “UFI Learning” training agreement, whose main areas of cooperation include the introduction of courses related to exhibitions and the provision of training for members of the Association.
10k race encourages “fascination” of cultures The popular contest “The FUNkiest Costume Award”, included in the program for the Sands 10K athletics race, which will take place on Sunday, will this year have the theme “The Fascination of Chinese and Western Cultures”, which will award prizes to the best looks. The competition encourages participants to incorporate allusive elements into their sports equipment, using, for example, traditional Chinese and Portuguese clothing items, or adopting a European style inspired by the Sands China resorts, “in order to highlight the unique fascination of Macau as a meeting point between China and the West”, according to a note from the Sports Institute. The main competition will start at 7:00 am at Praça do Lago Sai Van, ending at the Taipa Stadium. The Fun Run race starts at 7:40 am. Athletes can pick up the numbered bib with chip today and tomorrow, between 12pm and 9pm, and Saturday from 12pm to 10pm.

item.title: IAS will review subsidies to foster effectiveness and innovative capacity
item.description:
The review of the subsidy allocation regime to support the increase in the effectiveness of services and the formation of greater innovative capacity in social service institutions will be carried out this year by the Institute of Social Action. The organization also plans to launch a financing program for the acquisition of rehabilitation technologies. The president of the IAS, Hon Wai, stated during the spring lunch that social service is a “fundamental pillar for protecting the well-being of the entire population of the region and long-term social development”
VÍTOR REBELO
The Institute of Social Action (IAS) has as one of its goals for this year the review of the subsidy allocation regime, granted to “support the increase in the effectiveness of services and the formation of greater innovative capacity in social service institutions”. The review plan was one of the news transmitted yesterday by the president of the organization. Hon Wai, who was speaking at the spring lunch offered to workers in the social service sector, did not, however, provide details or a timetable on the matter. “With the decentralization of resources in social action and the reinforcement of support for vulnerable groups, we hope that social action measures will be effectively implemented and a solid base of support for the population's subsistence will be guaranteed”, he also said. He also considered “relevant” the increase in subsidy values ​​under the “Community Inclusion and Harmony Program”, “Special Support for the Three Types of Families in Vulnerable Situations” and “Allowance for Caregivers”. Furthermore, he recalled the implementation of the exemption from monthly fees for subsidized daycare centers for children from families in vulnerable situations and the free use of the “Peng On Tung” Telecare Service, operated on the fixed network and aimed at elderly people living alone or who are part of families with two elderly people, among other support measures. The president of the organization stressed, on the other hand, that the two Ten-Year Action Plans – for Support Services for the Elderly and for Rehabilitation Services – scheduled for this year “will be implemented in phases”. “We will implement measures to constantly raise the level of rehabilitation and support services for the elderly, and it should be noted that we will launch a financing program for the acquisition of rehabilitation technologies that aims to encourage the use of these technologies in social service institutions”, he also revealed during the speech. Hon Wai pointed out that, in 2026, the organization he directs and the services involved will cooperate in the launch of the “Full Cycle Health Protection Plan for Pregnant and Postpartum Women” and in carrying out various courses aimed at guardians, “with a view to creating a social support system that covers the entire cycle and all aspects of residents' lives”. Addressing the elderly care service, the person in charge mentioned that, with the coordination of more than 70 social service institutions, the “Survey and Registration of Isolated Elderly People and Families of Two Elderly People” is being carried out, “in order to establish a database that will serve as a tool for defining support measures for the elderly with greater precision”, and the creation of “15-Minute Service Circles for the Elderly”, “which could play an important role in the community.” Furthermore, he highlighted that the IAS has been attaching “a lot of importance to youth development”. With the support given to the “International Training Program for Anti-Drug Ambassadors for Young People of Macau” and the “International Affairs Training Program”, aimed at women, “we intend to build a sense of national identity and expand the horizons of young people in the Macao SAR, cultivate national values ​​such as love for the country and for Macau, helping them to develop their skills in the context of the development of the Macao SAR and the country”. In front of a full room at the Fisherman's Dock Convention and Exhibition Center, with around 600 guests, Hon Wai stated that social service “is, in fact, a fundamental pillar for protecting the well-being of the entire population of the region and long-term social development”. In relation to the challenges resulting from the low birth rate and the aging population, he assured that the IAS will continue to dedicate itself to the implementation of support measures in the areas of elderly and child care services. Regarding the youngest, the “Child Care Subsidy”, implemented for the first time, recalled that “aims to encourage birth rates and provide financial support to families with babies”, adding that “emotional education for children” was introduced in child care services and specific training is provided to daycare workers. Noting that this year marks the start of the Macao SAR's 3rd Five-Year Plan, Hon Wai considered that “it will be a crucial year to boost the region's social development in a new stage”.

item.title: Portuguese persecuted by inquisition walled up library in Badajoz in the 16th century
item.description:
The “hidden library of Barcarrota”, with books from the 16th century, discovered within the walls of a house near Badajoz, in Spain, in 1992, belonged to a Portuguese nobleman, Fernão Brandão, persecuted by the inquisition, according to a recently published study. The study “The hidden library of Barcarrota and the Portuguese nobleman Fernão Brandão”, by Pedro Martín Baños, PhD in Hispanian Philology, was recently published by the University of Extremadura and the Autonomous University of Barcelona, ​​both in Spain, and will be presented this Thursday in Badajoz. “This book is about the well-known ‘Hidden Library of Barcarrota’, a set of books of great historical and literary value” found in 1992 “hidden behind a wall of a house in Barcarrota, Badajoz, which date back to the 16th century and were considered heterodox, forbidden and magical”, explains Pedro Martín Baños, in a note about the book. The origin of the “hidden library of Barcarrota”, considered a “bibliographic treasure”, has been an enigma that the study now published believes to have unraveled. “The hypothesis defended in this work is, precisely, that the person hiding the walled books was the Portuguese nobleman Fernão Brandão, persecuted by the Inquisition in his country, who must have spent some time in Rome and then ended up in Barcarrota”, states Pedro Martín Baños. The library found in 1992 included 12 volumes, a manuscript and a decisive element for the identification of Fernão Brandão, a paper amulet that had inscribed “a dedication with a name, a place and a date: Fernão Brandão, from Évora; 23 April 1551. Rome, 23 April 1551”. Based on this inscription, Pedro Martín Baños focused his investigation on records with the name of Fernão Brandão and identified the Portuguese nobleman in the archives of the Inquisition of Évora, where he was the target of several accusations and complaints between 1547 and 1549, and later in Barcarrota, in documents related to property transfers in the locality. Fernão Brandão was accused by the Court of the Inquisition of Évora for “impiety and irreligiosity” and “sodomy”, for possessing “a book of sodomy”, lined as if it were religious, “in which men are represented riding against each other against nature from behind”. According to the accusations of the Portuguese Inquisition, cited in the study now published, they were that he ate fish and meat every Friday, Sunday and other religious festivals, never prayed, played football with the servants instead of going to mass, disappeared from the city during Lent to take refuge in a house in the countryside, did not confess, blasphemed against God and the saints and had small metal figures with which he practiced magic and sorcery rituals. Fernão Brandão went into exile in Castile “due to the excesses committed in his kingdom”, according to Portuguese genealogists cited in the study. The books in the “hidden library of Barcarrota”, purchased by the regional government of Extremadura in 1995, date back to the 16th century and are written in Spanish, Portuguese, French, Italian and Latin. The set includes books banned not only by the Inquisition in Portugal and Spain, such as an unknown edition of “Lazarillo de Tormes” (printed in Medina del Campo, Spain, in 1554), a copy of “Alborayque”, a satire on converted Jews, two treatises on palmistry, an exorcism manual, a work by Erasmus of Rotterdam, a book of prayers in several languages, a “Prayer of the Emparedada” in Portuguese and an erotic dialogue of a homosexual nature, “La Cazzaria”, by the Italian Antonio Vignali. The book now published in Spain suggests that Fernão Brandão walled up his library between the end of 1559 and the beginning of 1560, when the “Index of Prohibited Books of the Inquisitor Fernando de Valdés” was published in Spain. The list of inquisitor Fernando de Valdés, from 1559, was the first systematic index of the Spanish Inquisition and banned 698 works because they were considered heretical or immoral.
JTM/Lusa
